title: Phishing Response Playbook
content:
1. Block sending domain at email gateway.
2. Search mail logs for all recipients.
3. Force password resets for users who clicked.
4. Check auth logs for logins from suspicious IPs within 30 minutes of click events.
5. Send all-staff awareness alert within 2 hours.
6. Report domain to KE-CIRT and PhishTank.

KALRO note: Users are more likely to fall for emails spoofing IT support during maintenance windows.

title: Ransomware Containment and Recovery
content:
1. Isolate infected machine immediately — pull network cable.
2. Do NOT shut down — volatile memory may contain decryption keys.
3. Identify variant using ID Ransomware (upload ransom note).
4. Check NoMoreRansom.org for known decryptor before considering payment.
5. Check shared drives for encrypted files.
6. Restore from most recent clean backup — verify integrity first.
7. Reimage before reconnecting.

KALRO: Research backups on NAS at backup-nas-01. Snapshots every 6 hours. Contact David Kamau for access.

title: DDoS Mitigation — Upstream Filtering
content:
Safaricom Business provides upstream DDoS filtering under enterprise SLA.
1. Call Safaricom NOC 24/7 — get current number from ICT manager.
2. Provide account number, describe attack type and volume.
3. Request null-routing or scrubbing center activation.
4. Activation time: 15-30 minutes.
5. Monitor border router during mitigation.

Alternative: Enable Cloudflare Under Attack Mode if domain is proxied.

title: Compromised DB Credentials — Investigation
content:
1. Lock the compromised account immediately.
2. Pull 30 days of auth logs.
3. Check for data exfiltration — review SELECT queries on sensitive tables.
4. Check credential reuse across systems.
5. Interview account owner.
6. Preserve logs before remediation.
7. Escalate to HR and legal before confronting the user if insider threat suspected.

KALRO: Audit logs at /var/log/mysql/audit.log on hr-db-server-01. 90-day retention.

title: PowerShell Obfuscation Detection
content:
Indicators: Base64-encoded args, Invoke-Expression (IEX), download cradles.

1. Collect PowerShell transcript from machine.
2. Decode base64: [System.Text.Encoding]::Unicode.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String("..."))
3. Submit to VirusTotal.
4. Block C2 IP at firewall immediately.
5. Check for new scheduled tasks and registry run keys.

Preventive: Enable Constrained Language Mode on non-admin workstations via Group Policy.
